page: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128g-960s is a virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor clocked at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, operating on a shared CPU allocation model. Memory capacity is 128.0 GB, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core metrics, specifically within stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average tier. Memory performance is strong according to PassMark memory scores, though raw memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks register in the average tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong, placing in the top 25% of tested configurations.

The primary architectural tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core workloads compared to dedicated instances. However, this shared model provides high resource density, offering a large 128.0 GB memory footprint and 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage at a lower cost tier relative to dedicated memory-optimized hardware.

This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU execution. Appropriate use cases include in-memory databases, caching layers such as Redis, static web serving, and moderate database operations where the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast local NVMe storage can be leveraged efficiently.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-16c-128g-960s is an x86_64 virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. It features a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD and lacks a GPU. The processor includes a 32 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong memory and static web serving throughput. Multi-core CPU metrics and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff in sustained multi-core consistency but enables a cost-effective, high-density memory and storage profile. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and static web serving.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128g-960s is a virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128g-960s features 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized shared virtual server combining a 128 GB RAM capacity with 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 960 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong memory and static web serving benchmark results
Shared CPU allocation offering a cost-effective high-memory profile
Suited for caching layers, in-memory databases, and web serving
